question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2025

Code: 56/S/2 (set-2)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 33 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions carefully and follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 33 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. Section A - questions number 1 to 16 are multiple choice type questions. Each question carries 1 mark.
  4. Section B - questions number 17 to 21 are very short answer type questions. Each question carries 2 marks.
  5. Section C - questions number 22 to 28 are short answer type questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
  6. Section D - questions number 29 and 30 are case-based questions. Each question carries 4 marks.
  7. Section E - questions number 31 to 33 are long answer type questions. Each question carries 5 marks.
  8. There is no overall choice given in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in few questions in all the sections except Section -A.
  9. Kindly note that there is a separate question paper for Visually Impaired candidates.
  10. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.

SECTION A

Questions no. 1 to 16 are Multiple Choice type Questions, carrying 1 mark each. (16{tex}\times{/tex}1 = 16)
1.

When phenyl methyl ether is heated with HI, it produces:

a. Methyl chloride and Iodobenzene
b. Benzene and Methanol
c. Iodobenzene and Methanol
d. Phenol and Methyl iodide
1
2.

Out of the following organic compounds, the one which will react with Lucas reagent at room temperature is:

a. {tex}{CH}_{2}={CH}-{CH}_{2} {OH}{/tex}
b. {tex}{CH}_{3} {CH}_{2} {CH}_{2} {OH}{/tex}
c. {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{5} {CH}_{2} {OH}{/tex}
d. {tex}\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{3} {COH}{/tex}
1
3.

Which of the following amines has the highest boiling point?

a. 2, 2-Dimethyl propanamine
b. 3-Methylbutanamine
c. 2-Methylbutanamine
d. Pentanamine
1
4.

A compound CaCl2{tex}\cdot{/tex}6H2O undergoes complete dissociation in water. The Van't Hoff factor i is:

a. 9
b. 6
c. 3
d. 4
1
5.

Which one of the following halides contains {tex}{C}_{{sp^ 2}}-{X}{/tex} bond?

a. Alkyl halide
b. Allyl halide
c. Benzyl halide
d. Vinyl halide
1
6.

Identify the Freon from the following compounds:

a. {tex}{CCl}_{2} {~F}_{2}{/tex}
b. {tex}{CCl}_{2} {Br}_{2}{/tex}
c. {tex}{CH}_{2} {Cl}-{CHCl}_{2}{/tex}
d. {tex}{CHCl}_{2}-{CHCl}_{2}{/tex}
1
7.

Which of the following transition metals shows +1 and +2 oxidation states?

a. Mn
b. Zn
c. Sc
d. Cu
1
8.

The half-life period of a first-order reaction is 1386 seconds. The specific rate constant of the reaction is:

a. 5.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 s-1
b. 5.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 s-1
c. 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 s-1
d. 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 s-1
1
9.

The standard electrode potential of 'A', 'B' and 'C' are {tex}+0.68 {~V},-2.54 {~V}{/tex} and -0.50 V respectively. The order of their reducing power is:

a. {tex}{A} \gt {B} \gt {C}{/tex}
b. {tex}{A} \gt {C} \gt {B}{/tex}
c. {tex}{B} \gt {C} \gt {A}{/tex}
d. {tex}{C} \gt {B} \gt {A}{/tex}
1
10.

Silver nitrate is not stored in Zn vessel because:

a. Silver nitrate gets decomposed
b. {tex}{Ag}^{+}{/tex}gets reduced
c. Ag gets oxidised
d. Zn gets reduced
1
11.

Glucose on reaction with nitric acid gives:

a. Gluconic acid
b. Hexanoic acid
c. Saccharic acid
d. Glycolic acid
1
12.

The value of {tex}{K}_{{H}}{/tex} at 298 K for {tex}{Ar}({g}), {CO}_{2}({~g}), {HCHO}({g}){/tex} and {tex}{CH}_{4}({~g}){/tex} are {tex}40 \cdot 32{/tex}, 1.68, {tex}1.84 \times 10^{-5}{/tex} and 0.416 kbar respectively. When these gases are arranged in increasing order of solubility, the correct order is:

a. {tex}{HCHO} \lt {CH}_{4} \lt {CO}_{2} \lt {Ar}{/tex}
b. {tex}{HCHO} \lt {CO}_{2} \lt {CH}_{4} \lt {Ar}{/tex}
c. {tex}{Ar} \lt {CO}_{2} \lt {CH}_{4} \lt {HCHO}{/tex}
d. {tex}{Ar} \lt {CH}_{4} \lt {CO}_{2} \lt {HCHO}{/tex}
1

For Questions number 13 to 16, two statements are given - one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R). Select the correct answer to these questions from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
13.

Assertion (A): The molecularity of the reaction H2 + Br2 {tex}\to{/tex} 2HBr appears to be 2.
Reason (R): Two molecules of the reactants are involved in the given elementary reaction.

a. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
b. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
c. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
d. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
1
14.

Assertion (A): p-methoxyphenol is a stronger acid than p-nitrophenol.
Reason (R): Methoxy group shows -I effect whereas nitro group shows +I effect.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
15.

Assertion (A): Actinoids show wide range of oxidation states.
Reason {tex}(R){/tex}: This is due to comparable energies of {tex}5 {f}, 6 {~d}{/tex} and 7s orbitals.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
16.

Assertion (A): Presence of {tex}-{NO}_{2}{/tex} group at meta position increases the reactivity of haloarenes towards nucleophilic substitution reactions.
Reason(R): {tex}-{NO}_{2}{/tex} group decreases the electron density over the benzene ring and thus facilitates the attack of nucleophile on haloarenes.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

Define fuel cell. Give two advantages of fuel cell over ordinary cell.

  1. A dilute solution of {tex}{H}_{2} {SO}_{4}{/tex} with platinum electrodes
  2. An aqueous solution of {tex}{CuCl}_{2}{/tex} with platinum electrodes
 2
18.
  1. Name the reaction which can be used to distinguish chemically between aniline and {tex}{N}, {N}{/tex}-dimethylaniline.
  2. Why can aniline not be prepared by the ammonolysis of chlorobenzene under normal conditions?
 2
19.

Visha diluted the solutions of two electrolytes 'A' and 'B'. She observed that {tex}\Lambda_{{m}}{/tex} of 'A' increased 1.6 times whereas {tex}\Lambda_{{m}}{/tex} of 'B' increased 22 times. Which of the two is a weak electrolyte and why?

 2
20.

By giving reasons, explain that how the rate of reaction for a given reaction will be affected when:

  1. the temperature is increased.
  2. catalyst is added.
 2
21.

Give one point of difference between the following:

  1. {tex}\alpha{/tex}-helix and {tex}\beta{/tex}-pleated sheet structure of proteins
  2. Amylose and Amylopectin
 2

{tex}{Mg}({s}){/tex}{tex}\left|{Mg}^{2+}(0.001 {M}) \| {Cu}^{2+}(0.0001 {M})\right| {Cu}({s}){/tex}

24.

Give plausible explanation for each of the following:

  1. Sodium hydrogen sulphite is used for separation and purification of aldehydes.
  2. 2, 2, 6-trimethylcyclohexanone does not form cyanohydrin.
  3. Only one {tex}-{NH}_{2}{/tex} group of semicarbazide is involved in the formation of semicarbazone.
 3
25.(i)

Identify '{tex}A{/tex}' to '{tex}F{/tex}' in the following reaction sequence:

 3

OR
(ii)

What happens when:

  1. methyl chloride is treated with KCN?
  2. methyl bromide is treated with NaI in dry acetone?
  3. methyl bromide is treated with silver fluoride?
 3
26.
  1. Write IUPAC name of the complex {tex}\left[{Co}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)({CN})({en})_{2}\right]^{2+}{/tex}.
  2. Write the hybridization of {tex}\left[{Ni}({CO})_{4}\right]{/tex} and predict its magnetic behaviour.［Atomic number: {tex}{Ni}=28{/tex}]
  3. What type of isomerism is shown by {tex}\left[{Co}({en})_{3}\right]^{3+}{/tex}?
 3
27.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. Phenol is more acidic than alcohol.
  2. t-butyl bromide on reaction with sodium ethoxide gives alkene as main product and not ether.
  3. {tex}p{/tex}-nitrophenol is not steam volatile while {tex}o{/tex}-nitrophenol is steam volatile.
 3
28.

Methanamine reacts with benzoyl chloride to form a product 'P'. Give the structure and IUPAC name of 'P'. Can 'P' undergo Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction? If yes, then give the structure of the product. If no, then give reason.

 3

SECTION D

The following questions are case-based questions. Read the case carefully and answer the questions that follow.
29.

Reduce carbohydrates, increase protein to check diabetes, older people may need greater carbohydrates reduction. An average Indian derives {tex}61-64 \%{/tex} of energy from consumption of food rich in carbohydrates. A study published in the Journal of Diabetes Care has recommended reducing this to {tex}49-56 \%{/tex} for remission or prevention of Type 2 diabetes, one of the leading cause of death worldwide. Along with reducing carbohydrate intake, the study suggests that one should also increase protein intake up to {tex}14-20 \%{/tex} of the total energy consumption. Fat should contribute not more than {tex}21-27 \%{/tex} of the total energy consumption. In simple words, {tex}50 \%{/tex} of the plate should consist of fruits and green vegetables, 25% carbohydrates, {tex}25 \%{/tex} food rich in protein. Type 1 diabetes is due to deficiency of insulin.
Answer the following questions:

  1. Write any two functions of carbohydrates. (2)
  2. What are monosaccharides? (1)
    1. Write the name of the deficiency diseases caused due to the lack of Vitamin D. (1)

      OR

    2. Name the hormone which maintains the glucose level within narrow limit in the blood. (1)
 4
30.

One of the most distinctive properties of transition metal complexes is their wide range of colours. This means that some of the visible spectrum is being removed from white light as it passes through the sample, so the light that emerges is no longer white. The colour of the complex is complementary to that which is absorbed. The complementary colour is the colour generated from the wavelength left over. If green light is absorbed by the complex, it appears red. Table given below, gives the relationship between different wavelengths of the light absorbed and the colour observed.

Coordination Entity Wavelength of light absorbed (nm) Colour of light absorbed Colour of coordination entity
{tex}\left[{CoCl}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5}\right]^{2+}{/tex} 535 Yellow Violet
{tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)\right]^{3+}{/tex} 500 Blue-green Red
{tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}{/tex} 475 Blue Yellow-orange
{tex}\left[{Co}({CN})_{6}\right]^{3-}{/tex} 310 Ultra-Violet (U.V.) Pale Yellow
{tex}\left[{Cu}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{4}\right]^{2+}{/tex} 600 Red Blue
{tex}\left[{Ti}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}{/tex} 498 Blue-green Violet
  1. What is spectrochemical series? Write the relationship between {tex}\Delta_{{t}}{/tex} and {tex}\Delta_{0}{/tex}. (2)
    1. Arrange {tex}\left[{Co}({CN})_{6}\right]^{3-}{/tex}, {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}{/tex} and {tex}\left[{Cu}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{4}\right]^{2+}{/tex} in increasing order of wavelength of light absorbed. (1)

      OR

    2. On the basis of crystal field theory, write the electronic configuration of {tex}{d}^{5}{/tex} ion if {tex}\Delta_{0} \lt {P}{/tex}. (1)
  3. Define crystal field splitting energy. (1)
 4

SECTION E
31.(i)
  1. An organic compound 'P' {tex}\left({C}_{8} {H}_{10} {O}\right){/tex} on oxidation gave compound 'Q '. The compound 'Q' can also be obtained from a reaction of benzene with acetyl chloride in the presence of anhydrous {tex}{AlCl}_{3}{/tex}. '{tex}Q{/tex}' on treatment with {tex}{I}_{2}{/tex} in aq. NaOH gave '{tex}R{/tex}' and a yellow compound '{tex}S{/tex}'. Identify '{tex}P{/tex}', '{tex}Q{/tex}', '{tex}R{/tex}'and '{tex}S{/tex}'.
  2. Give the structure (s) of the product (s) when benzaldehyde is heated in the presence of conc. KOH.
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Carry out the following conversions:
    1. Propanone to Propane
    2. Acetophenone to Benzoic acid
    3. Benzyl chloride to Phenylethanoic acid
  2. Give a simple chemical test to distinguish between the following pairs of compounds:
    1. Benzaldehyde and Phenol
    2. Butanal and Butan-2-one
 5
32.(i)
  1. Write the chemical equations involved in the preparation of potassium permanganate from pyrolusite ore ({tex}{MnO}_{2}{/tex}).
  2. Give reasons for the following:
    1. {tex}{Sc}{/tex} (Atomic No. 21) salts are white.
    2. {tex}{E}^o_{\left.{Cu}^{2+}\right|{{Cu}} }{/tex} is positive whereas {tex}{E}_{\left.{Mn}^{2+}\right|{{Mn}}}^{0}{/tex} is negative.
    3. Transition metals have high enthalpies of atomisation.
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Complete and balance the following chemical equations:
    1. {tex}{Cr}_{2} {O}_{7}^{2-}({aq})+{H}_{2} {~S}({~g})+{H}^{+} \longrightarrow{/tex}
    2. {tex}{Cu}^{2+}({aq})+{I}^{-}({aq}) \longrightarrow{/tex}
  2. Give reasons:
    1. Separation of mixture of lanthanoids is difficult.
    2. Chromium has higher melting point than manganese.
    3. It is difficult to obtain oxidation state greater than two for Zn.
 5
33.(i)
  1. Define osmotic pressure. Why is it considered as a colligative property?
  2. The vapour pressure of two pure liquids A and B at 400 K are 450 and 700 mmHg respectively. Find out the composition of liquid mixture if the total vapour pressure of mixture is 600 mmHg.
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. What type of deviation from Raoult's law is expected when phenol and aniline are mixed with each other? What change in the net volume of the mixture and enthalpy is expected?
  2. Boiling point of water at 750 mmHg is {tex}99 \cdot 48^{\circ} {C}{/tex}. How much sucrose {tex}\left({M}=342 {~g} {~mol}^{-1}\right){/tex} is to be added to 500 g of water such that it boils at {tex}100^{\circ} {C}{/tex}? {tex}\left[{K}_{{b}}\right.{/tex} for water {tex}\left.=0.52 {~K} {~kg} {~mol}^{-1}\right]{/tex}
 5
